author: nflaig
content: <@755590043632140352> `should_build_on_full` as it's currently defined in alpha.8 is wrong (incomplete) as the proposer would consult the PTC votes even if head is not from previous slot but in that case we have attesters to determine full or empty and ptc votes shouldn't override attester votes?

author: potuz
content: Oh this is right. This also has to do with <@504202741933932544>'s comment in the reorg PR. Should build on full is meant to be used only when both full and empty have the same weight. Not sure what's the best way to fix it. Surely just returning true after the check for empty if the head is from an older slot is the easiest. And perhaps that's good enough

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5309 is what I had in mind

author: potuz
content: yeah, putting this not executable is even easier to avoid the awkwardness of finding out the head's slot. I would not write "we should build on full" explicitly though since we should build on whatever the payload status of head is.

author: potuz
content: hmm wait did this change? you **are** making it executable. But yeah, this works fine

author: potuz
content: I think you still want to remove the last part of the sentence <@586161934425128960> ", so we build on the *full* head"

author: nflaig
content: yeah jsut re-read that can remove it, do you have a good title for the PR, the CI doesn't like mine

author: nflaig
content: did you mean that we aren't even calling `should_build_on_full` if head is not from previous slot?

author: potuz
content: nah my first read was only of the comment part, for some reason I hadn't seen the extra check in the diff.

author: potuz
content: I thought your PR was purely not executable

author: potuz
content: As is now it's fine. We could go another route of explicitly calling get_weight

author: potuz
content: but I think this is better and has less edge cases

author: potuz
content: attesters will however favor late payloads in these scenarios

author: potuz
content: but I think even that is fine

author: nflaig
content: you mean related to my change?, if ptc voted not timely, then wouldn't the empty variant get more weight from attesters?

author: potuz
content: not because of your change, just that in general attesters follow payloads they have seen, regardless of timeliness. They only relax their view when the Proposer is trying to reorg. Anyway starting a thread on the forkchoice issue that may have happened on devnet cause I don't understand what Tuyen is saying in the issue.

author: nflaig
content: yeah, I updated https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5309 and added some description, let me know if that explains correctly what can happen, that's how I understand it, so proposer would be stuck in that case, I am not sure that's exactly what happened on the devnet right now, more a theoretical case

author: potuz
content: Looks good to me, I approved it, I don't know why I didn't include this clause directly. It's probably because of Prysm's pipeline that would only call this function only when building on top of previous slot.

author: nflaig
content: in https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5210 you left this note but we haven't merged or added that into the PR, I still haven't caught up on everything we merged last week (or end of prev week)

author: potuz
content: Yeah that PR is a placeholder until I get relevant feedback on that. Cc <@602753420033785856> you may want to weigh in. I'm leaning towards making this unconditional, that is always reorg the latest Payload if it's late and from the previous slot

author: sproul
content: I’ll take a look next week. Have you also seen https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/issues/5305?

author: sproul
content: main issue is separate, but I think the issues with should_build_on_full are the same ones from my comment there

author: potuz
content: Ah this doesn' t affect us cause we check the pending status for these reorgs and not the full/empty node. Yes I thought about the problem of having the parent being potentially split, but given that we only reorg to previous slot, there' s no way to actually measure this and made the executive decision of just counting pending

author: potuz
content: I left a reply in that repo, what I left there is what I think is the way all these functions should be meant to be used. Since most of this is optional I never touched that spec.

author: potuz
content: <@425572898787426305> please take a look at the last addition in that comment above. I think this is clear and we discussed this a few times, but perhaps this should be stressed in the engine API

author: barnabasbusa
content: bid.parent_block_hash is still derived from should_extend_payload(store, block.parent_root) while the actual build uses store,head so we can agree on old full case, but old empty would still diverge no?

author: nflaig
content: do you mean https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/gloas/validator.md?plain=1#L205, otherwise I am not sure what you are referencing

author: luca_zanolini
content:
Problems here would arise mainly from a stale/incorrect PTC signal, e.g. malicious/offline PTC members or late/uneven blob delivery causing a False data availability quorum? In the proofs in the document this does not show up because we assume timely payload/blob processing and an honest-online PTC majority, so the PTC should produce True/True when the builder reveals correctly.

If either of those fails, especially late blob delivery or faulty/offline PTC members, then we can enter the scenario you described. Or am I missing something?

author: potuz
content: nah the problem of using PTC is that it's simply incorrect, attesters have already made a decision in full when there are skipped slots or we have advanced more than one slot

author: potuz
content: so PTC should **only** be used when there's no difference in the weights for nodes, cause attesters haven't had time to vote for them

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5310 in case you meant that, seems like this was raised here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/874767108809031740/1506293536239390781 before, also do we still wanna move the whole function to validator.md? it seems fine in fork choice to me though
